text.title: Code Decomposition Principles Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns the audit catches before a decision record is acted on.

antipattern.symptom: Decision record carries no measured signals; rationale is 'feels too big'.
antipattern.root-cause: Agent skipped the scan step; emitted a record without running decomp-candidates.sh.
antipattern.fix: Reject. Run the scan; populate signals.loc / tokens / churn; re-draft.

antipattern.symptom: Split decision on a 400-LOC file that has 0 commits in 6 months.
antipattern.root-cause: Size-only heuristic. Cold files cost time to split for zero ongoing benefit.
antipattern.fix: Demote to leave unless churn is non-trivial; re-prioritise candidates by churn.

antipattern.symptom: Decomposition PR also changes logic — bug introduced in the move.
antipattern.root-cause: Agent combines mechanical move with semantic change.
antipattern.fix: Reject. Split into two PRs: decomposition (move only) + behaviour change (logic edit) — order matters.
Block. Force a single named owner before the record is reviewable.
